courses.title: English Language Essentials
courses.description: This course equips learners with essential English communication skills for academic, professional, and everyday contexts. It focuses on developing competence in listening, speaking, reading, writing, grammar, and vocabulary through interactive lessons and practical activities.

lessons.title: Importance of English Communication
lessons.text_content: Communication is one of the crucial life skills that helps humans interact, share ideas, feelings, and thoughts, resolve problems, and establish relationships. This module introduces learners to the importance of English communication and lays the basis for understanding how the language works through parts of speech.

lessons.title: Parts of Speech
lessons.text_content: Communication is one of the crucial life skills that helps humans interact, share ideas, feelings, and thoughts, resolve problems, and establish relationships. This module introduces learners to the importance of English communication and lays the basis for understanding how the language works through parts of speech.

questions.question_text: What is the main purpose of communication?
Which language is widely used for international communication?
Which of the following is a benefit of learning English?
Communication helps people:
Why is English important in education?

choices.choice_text: It is the language of many textbooks and research materials.
It is only used in schools.
It is only spoken in one country.

questions.question_text: In the sentence 'The teacher explained the lesson,' what part of speech is 'teacher'?
In the sentence 'She is happy today,' what part of speech is 'happy'?
In the sentence 'They run every morning,' what part of speech is 'run'?
In the sentence 'He speaks clearly,' what part of speech is 'clearly'?
In the sentence 'They completed the assignment,' what part of speech is 'assignment'?
A word that names a person, place, or thing is called a:
A word that replaces a noun is called a:
A word that describes a noun is called an:
A word that shows action is a:
A word that describes a verb is an:

lessons.title: Sentence Structure, Tenses, Punctuation & Capitalization
lessons.text_content: Grammar gives the rules for communication to be clear and meaningful. This module covers sentence structure, verb tenses, subject-verb agreement, punctuation, and capitalization.

questions.question_text: Which sentence is correct?

choices.choice_text: She went to school (referring to now).

questions.question_text: Which punctuation ends a question?
Which sentence uses capitalization correctly?
Choose the correct verb: 'The students _____ studying.'
Which sentence is complete?
Which is the correctly written version of: 'my brother likes football.'?
Which is the correctly written version of: 'she have two books.'?
Which is the correctly written version of: 'where are you going.'?
Which is the correctly written version of: 'the dogs barks loudly.'?
Which is the correctly written version of: 'i visited kenya last year.'?

lessons.title: Reading Strategies & Comprehension
lessons.text_content: Reading is a great way to learn, expand vocabulary, and develop critical thinking. This module covers reading strategies, identifying main ideas, and making inferences.

questions.question_text: What is the 'main idea' of a passage?

choices.choice_text: The central point the passage is making

questions.question_text: What are 'supporting details' in a text?

choices.choice_text: Facts and examples that back up the main idea

questions.question_text: What does it mean to 'make an inference' while reading?

choices.choice_text: Copying the text word for word
Drawing a conclusion based on clues in the text

questions.question_text: How can you determine the meaning of an unfamiliar word while reading?

choices.choice_text: Use context clues from surrounding sentences

questions.question_text: Why is identifying the main idea important when reading?

choices.choice_text: It helps learners understand and comprehend information more effectively

lessons.title: Paragraphs, Essays, Emails & Editing
lessons.text_content: Writing is an effective way of communicating in the classroom, workplace, and daily life. This module covers paragraph writing, essay writing, professional emails, report writing, editing and proofreading.

questions.question_text: What is the recommended word count for a short descriptive paragraph in this lesson?
What is the main goal of an essay such as 'The Importance of Education'?

choices.choice_text: To list random facts with no structure
To present a clear argument supported by reasoning and examples
To use as many big words as possible

questions.question_text: What should a professional email requesting information include?

choices.choice_text: A clear subject line and specific request details
Only a greeting with no request

questions.question_text: What is the purpose of editing and proofreading written work?

choices.choice_text: To correct grammar, punctuation, and spelling errors

questions.question_text: Which skill ensures written work is accurate and well presented?

lessons.title: Effective Speaking, Listening & Presentation Skills
lessons.text_content: Speaking and listening are basic communication abilities which affect personal, academic and professional success. This module builds confidence in speaking and enhances listening skills.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is an assessment criterion for effective speaking mentioned in this lesson?
Why do communication skills matter, according to this lesson?

choices.choice_text: They help express ideas clearly and understand others
They are only useful in school
They replace the need for listening
They are unimportant in the workplace

questions.question_text: When listening to an audio recording, what should learners identify first?
What is one key benefit of active listening mentioned in the module context?

choices.choice_text: It improves understanding and builds relationships
It replaces the need to speak

questions.question_text: Which combination of skills does this module aim to build?

courses.title: Entrepreneurship & Financial Literacy
courses.description: This course develops entrepreneurial thinking and financial management skills, enabling learners to identify opportunities, start businesses, manage finances, and make informed financial decisions.

lessons.title: What is Entrepreneurship?
lessons.text_content: Entrepreneurship is a key driver of economic growth, providing jobs, addressing societal challenges, and fostering innovation.

questions.question_text: Who is an entrepreneur?

choices.choice_text: A person who starts and manages a business
A person who only works for others
A person who avoids risk entirely

questions.question_text: Which characteristic is most important for entrepreneurs?
What is innovation?

choices.choice_text: Introducing new ideas, products, or methods

questions.question_text: Which is an example of a business opportunity?

choices.choice_text: Opening a business that meets customer needs

questions.question_text: Why is creativity important for entrepreneurs?

choices.choice_text: It helps develop unique products and solve problems
It has no impact on business

questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT one of the five characteristics of successful entrepreneurs listed in this lesson?
What best describes the difference between creativity and innovation?

choices.choice_text: Creativity is generating new ideas; innovation is turning those ideas into practical products or services
They mean exactly the same thing
Creativity only applies to art, not business

lessons.title: Career Opportunity & Entrepreneurship as a Pathway
lessons.text_content: Career development encompasses making informed choices in employment, self-employment, and continuing career development throughout life.

quiz.title: Career Opportunity and Entrepreneurial Pathway — Quiz

questions.question_text: What is a career opportunity?

choices.choice_text: A chance to obtain employment or grow professionally
A one-time task with no future value

questions.question_text: Which of the following is a benefit of entrepreneurship?
What best describes the difference between employment and self-employment?

choices.choice_text: Employment means working for an employer for wages, while self-employment means running your own business
They are identical in every way
Self-employment means working for a government agency only
Employment always pays more than self-employment

questions.question_text: Why might learners consider entrepreneurship as a career path?

choices.choice_text: It provides independence, income, and opportunities to create jobs
It guarantees instant wealth with no effort
It requires no skills or planning
It is only suitable for large corporations

questions.question_text: Reflecting on personal interests and goals when choosing a career path is an example of what?

lessons.title: Budgeting, Saving, Banking Services & Credit
lessons.text_content: Financial literacy is the ability to understand and effectively use financial information to make informed decisions about managing money, including budgeting, saving, banking, and credit.

questions.question_text: What is a budget?
Which banking service keeps money safe?
What is saving?

choices.choice_text: Setting aside money for future use

questions.question_text: Which of the following is an example of credit?
Why is financial planning important?
What is the main purpose of saving?

choices.choice_text: To prepare for future needs and emergencies

questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines financial literacy?

choices.choice_text: The ability to understand and manage money effectively through budgeting, saving, banking, and informed decisions
The ability to memorize bank account numbers
The ability to avoid all forms of banking
The ability to spend without a plan

questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT a benefit of budgeting?
Which of the following is a banking service?
What is the key difference between saving and borrowing?

choices.choice_text: Saving means setting aside money for future use, while borrowing means receiving money from a lender to be repaid, usually with interest
Borrowing always earns interest for the borrower

questions.question_text: Given a monthly income of $500, which budget allocation best reflects a balanced plan covering rent, food, transport, savings, and emergency fund?

choices.choice_text: Rent $150, Food $120, Transport $50, Savings $100, Emergency Fund $80
Rent $400, Food $50, Transport $50, Savings $0, Emergency Fund $0
Rent $0, Food $500, Transport $0, Savings $0, Emergency Fund $0
Rent $250, Food $250, Transport $0, Savings $0, Emergency Fund $0

lessons.title: SMART Goals & Personal Goal Setting
lessons.text_content: Personal goals give direction, motivate, and provide a measure of success. This module covers goal setting, SMART goals, and short/long-term goal planning.

questions.question_text: What is a personal goal?

choices.choice_text: A target an individual wants to achieve
A rule imposed by others with no personal input

questions.question_text: What does SMART stand for in goal setting?
Which of the following is an example of a short-term goal?

choices.choice_text: Complete my accounting course this semester
Become a nationally recognized expert over a lifetime

questions.question_text: Which of the following is an example of a long-term goal?

choices.choice_text: Become a successful lawyer and advocate
Reply to an email this afternoon

questions.question_text: Which of the following goals best follows SMART principles?

choices.choice_text: I will save $50 every month for the next 12 months to start my own business.
I want to be rich someday.
I will try to save some money eventually.
I want to have more money in general.

lessons.title: Identifying, Evaluating & SWOT-Analyzing Business Ideas
lessons.text_content: All successful businesses start with a sound business idea and real market opportunities. This module covers evaluating business ideas, including SWOT analysis.

questions.question_text: What is a business opportunity?

choices.choice_text: A chance to start a profitable business
A type of loan repayment plan

questions.question_text: Which is a characteristic of a good business idea?

choices.choice_text: It meets customer needs and is profitable

questions.question_text: What does SWOT stand for?
Which factor most influences the choice of a business idea?
Why should entrepreneurs evaluate business ideas before starting?

choices.choice_text: To determine their feasibility and potential success
To make the process take longer with no benefit
To avoid ever starting a business

questions.question_text: In a SWOT analysis for a small clothing business, which of the following would be classified as a 'Threat'?

choices.choice_text: Strong competition and rising costs of materials

questions.question_text: In the same SWOT analysis, which of the following would be classified as an 'Opportunity'?

choices.choice_text: Growing online market and increasing demand for fashion
